title: Bulk email unsubscribe/opt out option

posts.author: matttlap (Matthew Laperriere)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi-

We’re sending bulk emails and we need to include an unsubscribe option. The end result would need to be something that we can filter for to avoid sending emails to that contact in the future.

For example, if we had something that recepients could click or do that would change an item’s status to “unsubscribed”, that would be perfect.

Is there any way to do this? It’s a legal issue, so I would assume Monday has a way to address it…right?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Matthew,

Thank you for flagging this. I hear you and have raised this with our dedicated product team internally.

Transparently, I don’t believe we have a specific opt-out feature built into the solution just yet, however as mentioned, I’ve reached out to the feature owner and will get back to you asap!

posts.author: matttlap (Matthew Laperriere)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks!

I suppose we could link to a form that would ask for the email someone wanted to block. But that’s a somewhat clunky process and wouldn’t be ideal if there was a typo or error on either side (which isn’t far fetched when you have thousands of contacts to consider).

The best case would just be a button they could click to unsubscribe, like most bulk emails have.

posts.author: Gabriel_Marco (Gabriel Marco)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be perfect. I also use it to send emails and need this to be ok with regulations.

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can already achieve something like this using Monday Forms.

All you would need is to embed an unsubscribe link in your mass emails (which should be possible as Mass Emails allow HTML formatting)

posts.author: Gabriel_Marco (Gabriel Marco)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would I need to create a new board to do this?
I mean, can I create a form on my main board where user only fill his email and I can change status o do I have to do it in another board?

posts.author: Downing510 (Matt Downing)
posts.cookedHtml: If we were to set up an unsubscribe form like the one you mention, how would you suggest honoring that request in the Contacts board? Would you have to remove their email manually, or is there another creative way to automate this so the next time you send a group email, you do not include them?
